title: Publisher Buffers, Drain, Admission, and Bounded Batching · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent integration team receives different traffic, tenant, topology, schema, and failure constraints and transfers publisher buffers, drain, admission, and bounded batching into a new publisher admission and buffer-pressure controller. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of publisher admission and buffer-pressure controller with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Treat amqplib publish and sendToQueue return values as writable-buffer admission signals rather than delivery confirmations or Promises.
Pause admission after false, coordinate a bounded drain waiter, handle close and abort, and avoid lost wakeups or listener leaks.
Propagate publisher capacity to HTTP or job admission with queue, byte, time, tenant, and rejection budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Publish Boolean Contract
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 5667 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to propagate blocked, byte capacity, publish boolean, and drain state into upstream admission; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat amqplib publish and sendToQueue return values as writable-buffer admission signals rather than delivery confirmations or Promises.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Publish Boolean Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for treat amqplib publish and sendtoqueue return values as writable-buffer admission signals rather than delivery confirmations or promises. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Publish Boolean Contract, keep Publish Boolean Contract connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.
